title: Sharing the Road
description: Bicycles, motorcycles, big trucks, and work zones.

scenarios.prompt: You are passing a person on a bicycle. How much room should you give?

feedback.correct: Yes — give a bicycle at least 3 feet of space when you pass.
feedback.inches: That's too close. Give a bicycle at least 3 feet. Try again.
feedback.none: Bicycles need room — at least 3 feet when passing. Try again.
feedback.default: Give a bicycle at least 3 feet of space. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: A big truck is next to you. Where is it most dangerous to drive?

choices.label: Right beside it, where the driver can't see you
Far in front, where it can see you

feedback.correct: Yes — trucks have big blind spots on the sides. If you can't see the driver's mirror, they can't see you.
feedback.far: Being well in front where the driver can see you is safer. The danger is right beside the truck. Try again.
feedback.behind: Staying back with space is safe. The danger zone is right alongside the truck. Try again.
feedback.default: The danger is beside the truck, in its blind spot. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: You see orange signs and workers ahead. What should you do?

choices.label: Slow down and watch for workers

feedback.correct: Yes — slow down in work zones and watch for people. Fines are often higher here too.
feedback.same: Slow down in a work zone — people are working close to the road. Try again.
feedback.fast: Never speed up in a work zone. Slow down and stay alert. Try again.
feedback.default: Slow down and watch for workers. Try again.
